item.text: The reads are still returning. Strategically, P1 is ahead on centers and units, so I’m looking for a turn that removes exposed raiders while converting income into another recruit.

The current files confirm P2 used its last turn to flip southeast and kill the wounded west raider. I’m preparing a counterstrike that uses Training’s attack upgrade plus one deck damage to kill the upgraded southeast raider, then uses the remaining pressure to flip southeast back and recruit from P1’s center income.

One constraint matters here: captures happen during movement, before attacks, so P1 cannot flip a center by moving onto an enemy-occupied southeast hex and then killing that enemy. I’m prioritizing kills and recruits instead; the center can be reclaimed after the blocker is removed.
The action files are written. I’m running deck resolution now, then I’ll check whether the western raider path to support the marksman is legal.
Deck resolution succeeded. I’m running board resolution now; if any path is too long, I’ll correct the smallest part of the board action file.
Board resolution accepted the full line, including both raider kills and two recruits. I’m inspecting the resolved output before commit so the timeline entry is precise.

The resolved turn removed both forward P2 raiders and leaves P1 with a large unit lead after recruitment. I’m committing with strict win checks; if the runner requires a pending win-event artifact, I’ll use its expected JSON exactly.

item.command: /bin/zsh -lc "bun run --silent playtest -- commit-turn --run ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01 --deck-result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-011.deck.result.json --board-result ../runs/codex-rush-vs-engine-01/results/turn-011.board.result.json --summary \"P1 played Blast, Training, and Storm, bought nothing at 2 money, upgraded a raider, killed both forward P2 raiders, and recruited two raiders.\" --reasoning \"P1 had no useful trash because the action payload cards were needed and trashing Copper would reduce an already-low buy turn. Blast kept the action chain alive, Training provided a permanent attack upgrade, and Storm added enough damage budget to finish exposed raiders under the per-attacker damage cap. No buy was available at 2 money that improved the deck. On board, P1 upgraded the raider moving to center-east, then used that raider plus 1 deck damage to kill P2's upgraded raider on center-southeast. P1 also sent the central raider west to attack P2's raider on center-west-south for 7 damage, with the marksman moving into range to finish it. The home raider moved out to open a spawn. P1 controlled six centers at income, reached 13 supply, and recruited two raiders, creating a major unit-count threat for P2 to answer on the next turn.\" --strict-win"

item.text:
Completed `turn-011` for P1 and validated the run strictly.

P1 played `blast`, `training`, and `storm`, bought nothing at 2 money, upgraded `P1-raider-turn-007-a`, killed both forward P2 raiders, and recruited two raiders.

Validation passed:

`Valid replay bundle: Current best baseline playthrough (11 entries)`
